name: Boumort and Carreu Valley Loop
name_original: Sierra de Boumort y Valle de Carreu — Ruta circular por el Pallars Jussá
description: Tackle a difficult 36.5-mile mountain biking loop through the Sierra de Boumort y Valle de Carreu, gaining 5619 feet with stunning views.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Conca De Dalt offers a diverse landscape characterized by rugged terrain, significant elevation changes, and varied natural features. The region, nestled in the Lleida Pre-Pyrenees, features steep slopes, dramatic ravines, and towering cliffs, particularly in areas like Collegats. Mountain bike trails frequently traverse both valleys and mountains, providing a dynamic riding experience through the Noguera Pallaresa valley and around the Sant Antoni reservoir. This varied topography creates an engaging environment for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Conca De Dalt The most popular mountain bike trail is Sierra de Boumort y Valle de Carreu — Ruta circular por el Pallars Jussá, a 36.5 miles (58.7 km) trail that takes 6 hours 17 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the extensive Boumort National Game Reserve, known for its parallel mountain ranges. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Salàs de Pallars y Ermita de Sant Salvador de Toralla — Circular por el Pallars Jussà desde La Pobla de Segur, a difficult 25.7 miles (41.4 km) path. This route guides riders through areas featuring historical sites like hermitages and offers views of the surrounding Pallars Jussà landscape. Local mountain bikers also love the Cingles de Pessonada – Ascent in Conca loop from la Pobla de Segur, a 29.2 miles (47.0 km) trail leading through the Cingles de Pessonada, often completed in about 4 hours 48 minutes. Mountain biking around Conca De Dalt is defined by its rugged mountains, deep ravines, and the presence of the Sant Antoni reservoir. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate climbs to challenging technical descents. The routes in Conca De Dalt are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 270 reviews. More than 5000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Conca De Dalt's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Conca De Dalt?
A: Conca De Dalt offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 130 routes available for exploration. These range from easy rides to challenging technical descents.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Conca De Dalt?
A: The region is characterized by rugged and varied landscapes, including steep slopes, dramatic ravines, and towering cliffs, particularly in areas like Collegats. Trails frequently traverse both valleys and mountains, offering a dynamic riding experience through the Noguera Pallaresa valley and around the Sant Antoni reservoir. You'll encounter diverse terrain from moderate climbs to technical rocky sections.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly mountain bike trails in Conca De Dalt?
A: Yes, Conca De Dalt has options for all skill levels. There are 20 easy mountain bike routes, perfect for families or those looking for a more relaxed ride. Additionally, 67 moderate trails offer a good balance of challenge and accessibility.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Conca De Dalt?
A: You can encounter a variety of natural beauty and historical sites. Highlights include the unique karstic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2472984" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Montcortés&lt;/a&gt;, the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5269223" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collegats Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2698547" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L'Argenteria Cliffs and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also offer panoramic views of the Sant Antoni reservoir and pass through protected natural areas like the Boumort National Game Reserve.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that offer views of the Sant Antoni reservoir?
A: Yes, several routes provide stunning views of the Sant Antoni reservoir. A notable moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6954687" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Pantano de San Antonio – La Coma Trail loop from Sant Pere de les Maleses&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 53.4 km and takes approximately 4 hours 20 minutes.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Conca De Dalt?
A: The mountain bike trails in Conca De Dalt are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 270 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the stunning natural scenery, and the engaging challenges offered by the routes.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that pass by historical sites or villages?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in cultural heritage. For example, the moderate route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24316817" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pobla de Segur – Claverol Castle loop from la Pobla de Segur&lt;/a&gt;, at 28.5 km, guides you past historical sites like Claverol Castle. You can also explore the charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5164268" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salàs de Pallars Historic Village&lt;/a&gt;, which is featured on some routes.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Conca De Dalt?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, the Lleida Pre-Pyrenees region generally offers pleasant conditions for mountain biking during spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder. Summer can be warm, especially in the valleys, while winter may bring colder temperatures and snow at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any technical singletrack trails for experienced mountain bikers?
A: Yes, Conca De Dalt is known for its diverse and technical trails. The region research mentions exciting trails like the 'Serengueti Trail' for descents and the 'Roques Pelades Trail' for its technical rocky sections and exhilarating singletrack experiences, catering to more advanced riders.

Q: Can I find mountain bike routes that are circular?
A: Many of the mountain bike routes in Conca De Dalt are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. This includes routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6954687" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Pantano de San Antonio – La Coma Trail loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24316817" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pobla de Segur – Claverol Castle loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike route available in Conca De Dalt?
A: Among the featured routes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e950317969/sierra-de-boumort-y-valle-de-carreu-ruta-circular-por-el-pallars-jussa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Boumort y Valle de Carreu — Ruta circular por el Pallars Jussá&lt;/a&gt; is one of the longest, spanning 58.7 km and typically taking around 6 hours 17 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the extensive Boumort National Game Reserve.
tips: Small stone shelter. It is located in the Sierra de Boumort, near the homonymous peak. It's basically a survival shelter.
Very quiet place to rest and recover energy. We find the Mint fountain and a picnic area surrounded by nature

Located in the center of the town and under the imposing Roca de Pessonada, in the Comarca del Pallars Jussà, we find the Church of Santa Maria.
As a curiosity, his Romanesque paintings are preserved, today, in the Museum of Fine Arts of Bilbao.
Date of 1982

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Sierra de Boumort y Valle de Carreu circular route?
faqs.answer: The starting point for this circular route is typically accessible by car, though specific parking areas are not detailed in the provided information. It's advisable to check local maps or komoot's route planner for the most convenient parking near the trailhead in the Pallars Jussá region.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to tackle this route is generally during spring and autumn. Winters can bring snow, making parts of the trail impassable, while summers can be very hot, which can be challenging given the route's difficulty and length.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to mountain bike in the Boumort National Hunting Reserve?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Boumort is a protected natural space and a National Hunting Reserve. While general access for mountain biking is usually permitted, it's always best to check with local authorities or the reserve management for any specific regulations, permits, or fees that might apply to recreational activities within the reserve, especially for larger groups or specific areas. There are bird-watching hides available for a fee and with permission.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Given that the route traverses the Boumort National Hunting Reserve, which is home to abundant wildlife, it is highly recommended to keep dogs on a leash at all times to protect the local fauna. Always check specific reserve regulations regarding dogs before your visit.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is diverse and challenging. It primarily consists of tracks, but these are often very stony with significant elevation changes. While ascents are generally long, sustained, and rideable, descents feature abundant, less technical singletracks. Be prepared for some technical sections that require advanced riding skills. You'll pass through pine forests, oak woods, and some fir zones.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see along the trail?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Boumort is renowned for its rich wildlife. It's a National Hunting Reserve and one of the few places in Europe where you can observe all four species of European scavengers: griffon vulture, cinereous vulture, bearded vulture, and Egyptian vulture. You're also likely to spot large populations of deer and roe deer.

faqs.question: What points of interest will I encounter on the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll pass several notable points. These include the Refugio de Boumort, the Fuente de la Menta, and the historic Church of Santa Maria de Pessonada. The trail also offers magnificent views of Collegats and the deep Carreu valley.

faqs.question: What level of fitness and riding skill is required for this route?
faqs.answer: This route is consistently rated as 'difficult'. It demands a very good fitness level and advanced riding skills due to its length (around 58 km) and significant elevation gain (over 1,700 meters). While 100% ridable, some challenging sections may require experienced riders to push their bikes.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: Given its length of approximately 58.7 km and over 1,700 meters of elevation gain, this route typically takes around 6 hours and 17 minutes of pure riding time. However, allow for additional time for breaks, enjoying the views, and potential technical sections.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route utilizes sections of several official trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the Camí de Carreu, Pista de Boumort, Pista d'Hortoneda, and Claverol, among others. It also briefly touches on the Volta Sant Corneli and TransCatalunya. De Ponent trails.

faqs.question: Are there places to refill water or get supplies along the way?
faqs.answer: The route passes by the Refugio de Boumort, which might offer water or basic supplies depending on its operating status. Additionally, the Fuente de la Menta provides a natural water source. However, it's always wise to carry sufficient water and snacks for the entire duration of this challenging ride, as services can be limited in this wild area.

waypoints.name: Church of Santa Maria de Pessonada
